title: Symplectic homology for cobordisms
speaker: Alexandru Oancea
affiliation: Université Pierre et Marie Curie; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: Symplectic homology for a Liouville cobordism (possibly filled at the negative end) generalizes simultaneously the symplectic homology of Liouville domains and the Rabinowitz-Floer homology of their boundaries. I intend to explain a conceptual framework within which one can understand it, and give a sample application which shows how it can be used in order to obstruct cobordisms between contact manifolds. Based on joint work with Kai Cieliebak and Peter Albers.

proof_stages.label: Liouville cobordism framework and directed questions
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes Liouville cobordisms as exact symplectic manifolds with positive and negative contact-type boundary, then uses standard sources of such cobordisms to motivate directed questions about contact cobordism, singularity adjacency, and Liouville-domain embeddings.

proof_stages.label: Known overtwisted and hypertight boundary phenomena
proof_stages.summary: The speaker recalls that overtwisted negative boundaries are Weinstein-unobstructed under the expected topological hypotheses, while a hypertight negative boundary cannot cobord to an overtwisted positive boundary by a Niederkrüger/Bishop-disk compactness argument.

proof_stages.label: Main obstruction theorem and need for cobordism SH
proof_stages.summary: The joint theorem to be proved rules out a Liouville cobordism with hypertight negative end and positive end whose symplectic homology is defined and vanishes, and nonfillable hypertight examples explain why ordinary filling-based symplectic homology is not enough.

proof_stages.label: Definition of symplectic homology for a cobordism
proof_stages.summary: Under hypotheses such as hypertightness, fillability, or index bounds on the negative boundary, \(SH(W)\) is defined from Floer homology of completed-cobordism Hamiltonians by taking slope limits and then action-window limits, with positive, zero, and negative action generators giving several truncated flavors.

proof_stages.label: Unital product and order of limits
proof_stages.summary: Pair-of-pants products on truncated Floer complexes make \(SH(W)\) a unital ring, but only when the lower action bound is sent to \(-\infty\) before the upper action bound is sent to \(+\infty\).

proof_stages.label: Relative functoriality and transfer maps
proof_stages.summary: The cobordism groups fit into relative long exact sequences for stacked sub-cobordisms and admit unital ring transfer maps, so a cobordism relates the two boundary theories indirectly rather than by a direct map between them.

proof_stages.label: Exact-truncation diagram proof of the obstruction
proof_stages.summary: Using the exact sequences for \(SH\), \(SH^{\ge0}\), and \(SH^{>0}\) for both \(W\) and its positive boundary, plus the \(c_1=0\) grading needed for injectivity at the zero-action level, vanishing of the positive-boundary theory forces the unit in \(W\) to be killed by positive-action data, which would require a contractible Reeb orbit on the hypertight negative boundary.

examples.name: Links of isolated singularities under perturbation
examples.note: Perturbing an isolated singularity produces a Weinstein cobordism from links of simpler singularities to the original link, explicitly motivating the guiding idea that complexity increases from negative to positive boundary.

examples.name: Nonfillable hypertight torus contact structures and higher-dimensional analogues
